June 10, 1992
MEMORANDUM
To: 
Ernie Scott, Councilman 
/
From: 
C.R. Martin
Re: 
Conflicts/Request
You have asketl us to report to you regarding u porribl" conflict of interest as
outlined in your letter dated May 21, 1992, which is attached hereto requesting guidance.
Please be advised:
1. Only the FPPC is authorized to give advice concerning G.C. 87100 and
con{licts of interest thereunder; although you may share the information in
this Memo, it should be done only with the explicit understanding that you
should not rely upon the opinions expressed, but should use this
information only to obtain a formal opinion from the FPPC.
2. 
You state that both before and after you election to office, you operated a
real estate brokers' business in Victorville more than 2 miles outside of the
City of Adelanto. In connection with such business, you had an on-going
relationship with an investor in [.os Angeles, and for a commission or
equity position, helped the investor to buy and hold land interests within
Adelanto's Sphere of Influence (but not within the City itself). You want
to know whether your relationship with the invcstor, his commission
earnings, or your equity positions place you in a present or potential
conliicts situation.
3. 
As you are well aware, the two elements of a conflict under 87100 are:
(a) an interest of some kind, i.e., land, loan, business, investment etc., and
(b) participation in the governmental process which has or will have some
impact upon that interest.


Scott, Ernie
June 8, 1992
Page 2
RZClcRM/jh
4.
There can be little doubt that you could have such an "interest'with
the investor, be it an on-going business relationship, investment or other
(query re investments in a sphere of influence as to distance from
boundary); and it is such that if the second element were to be present, a
possible conllicts situation could arise. 
/
As to the second element (i.e. "participation"), you have been on the
Council since mid-April, and no indication exists that he has in any way
participated in matters which could impact such interests. 'Participation"
means voting, influencing or othenvise affecting a governmental action
regarding such interests.
Therefore, we surmise on the basis of the information which you have
presented that there is no present conllict under 87100, but that the actual
nature of the "interests" and location thereof be presented to FPPC
together with a request for definition of "participation", so that you
may be properly advised concerning potential conllicts during his term.
5.

sections.question_synthetic: Councilman Ernest Scott seeks FPPC guidance on whether his real estate business activities and financial interests related to an investor's property acquisitions in Adelanto's sphere of influence constitute a conflict of interest under California Government Code Section 87100.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is an incoming request/transmittal letter, not an FPPC response. Contains Scott's own detailed disclosure memorandum dated May 31, 1992, plus a preliminary city attorney memo (June 10) providing non-binding analysis before formal FPPC request. No formal CONCLUSION/ANSWER section from FPPC present. The city attorney advises that only FPPC can provide authoritative guidance.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Councilman Ernest Scott seeks FPPC guidance on whether his real estate business activities and financial interests related to an investor's property acquisitions in Adelanto's sphere of influence constitute a conflict of interest under California Government Code Section 87100.

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request package forwarding Councilman Ernest Scott's conflict of interest inquiry to the FPPC. The city attorney transmits Scott's detailed memorandum disclosing business relationships with investor Hank Ou, including a $30,000 commission/5% ownership interest in a 160-acre property acquisition, and requests formal FPPC guidance on potential conflicts of interest under Government Code Section 87100.
